title: Merge Bases, Topology, and Integration Strategies · Collaboration Transfer
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer merge bases, topology, and integration strategies into a new merge topology and strategy decision record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of merge topology and strategy decision record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.
Choose fast-forward, no-ff, ff-only, or refusal from release policy and graph evidence rather than branch-name folklore.
Evaluate ort strategy behavior and bounded options for renames, whitespace, subtree shifts, and favoring conflict hunks without replacing semantic review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G98 is a lab in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-merge-result-defense-1hxz4p" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Merge Result Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-merge-result-defense-1hxz4p
# scenario-key: trace-1hxz4p probe-p4zxh1 boundary-xz4p1h owner-1hxz4px fault-1hxz4pz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1h candidate=b00b14p
# hypothesis: criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor
git merge-base --all release topic
# expected: all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed
# observation: two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration
# changed-case: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base
# after-ref: main=a11ce1h candidate=c0ffeep
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy
# transfer-limit: validate resulting behavior in an authorized disposable clone because graph evidence is not semantic proof
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
